methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Returns the specified property group from the collection using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Returns the specified property group from the collection using the unique ID of the property group. The ID is consistent and can't be modified by the user and isn't affected by localization.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the specified PropertyGroup using the name of the group.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of properties within the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Library Item API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to examine library items using the API.
To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the existing default code. The script will search for and record all components and library items in the current project. They are displayed in a dialog when the script has finished.

classType.title: PropertyGroups.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: PropertyGroups.item Method
item.description: Returns the specified property group from the collection using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the property within the collection where the first item is 0.

itemById.title: PropertyGroups.itemById Method
itemById.description: Returns the specified property group from the collection using the unique ID of the property group. The ID is consistent and can't be modified by the user and isn't affected by localization.
itemById.return_description: Returns the specified PropertyGroup or null if the ID doesn't match a group within the collection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique ID of the property group.

related_samples.name: Library Item API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to examine library items using the API.
To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the existing default code. The script will search for and record all components and library items in the current project. They are displayed in a dialog when the script has finished.

itemByName.title: PropertyGroups.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the specified PropertyGroup using the name of the group.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified group or null if the name doesn't match a group within the collection.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the group to return. This is the name as seen in the user interface. Not all groups have a name.

count.title: PropertyGroups.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of properties within the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = propertyGroups_var.count

isValid.title: PropertyGroups.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = propertyGroups_var.isValid

objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

propertyValue = propertyGroups_var.objectType
